perturbation.original_text: Payment of the Royalty shall be made no later than 45 days after the termination of the preceding full calendar quarter.
perturbation.changed_text: Payment of the Royalty shall be made no later than 30 days after the termination of the preceding full calendar quarter.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment deadline for royalties. Section 3(b) now states that royalty payments are due within '30 days' after the quarter's end, conflicting with the original '45 days' timeframe. This inconsistency creates uncertainty about when payments are actually due, potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Royalty owed Theismann shall be calculated on a quarterly calendar basis ("Royalty Period") commencing on the first (1st) day of June, September, December, and March, except that the first and last calendar quarters may be "short" depending on the effective date of this Agreement. Payment of the Royalty shall be made no later than 30 days after the termination of the preceding full calendar quarter. The foregoing notwithstanding, if this Agreement is terminated in accordance with the provisions herein during a Royalty Period, the Royalty shall be calculated up to and including the Termination Date and payment of the Royalty shall be made as provided herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the royalty payment deadline from 45 days to 30 days after the end of the quarter. The contradicted text states, 'Payment of the Royalty shall be made no later than 30 days after the termination of the preceding full calendar quarter.' While both texts now specify 30 days, the change from the original 45 days introduces a contradiction with the original text of Section 3(b) before the change, leading to an inconsistency regarding the payment deadline. Therefore, there's a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In this regard, Bizzingo agrees to provide Joseph Theismann with at least thirty (30) days' written notice of any photographic sessions or public appearances to permit Theismann to properly schedule the session or appearance.
perturbation.changed_text: In this regard, Bizzingo agrees to provide Joseph Theismann with at least fifteen (15) days' written notice of any photographic sessions or public appearances to permit Theismann to properly schedule the session or appearance.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by modifying the notice period for promotional appearances. Section 4 now states that Bizzingo will provide Joseph Theismann with at least '15 days' notice for appearances, conflicting with the original '30 days' timeframe. This creates ambiguity about the required notice period, potentially leading to scheduling conflicts and disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition to the other terms and conditions herein, during the Term, Theismann also will; Bizzingo recognizes that Theismann's schedule and will not schedule any such session or appearance at a time that would conflict with Theismann's current obligations. Following the execution of this Agreement, Theismann will provide Bizzingo with an availability schedule indicating black out and/or available dates and from time to time will endeavor to update such schedule. In this regard, Bizzingo agrees to provide Joseph Theismann with at least fifteen (15) days' written notice of any photographic sessions or public appearances to permit Theismann to properly schedule the session or appearance. Bizzingo will pay all reasonable out of pocket expenses incurred by Theismann in connection with such session or appearance which expenses shall not exceed One Thousand Dollars ($1,000) per day unless Theismann receives prior written approval from Bizzingo. For any travel incurred by Theismann as part of his appearances, Bizzingo will provide first class air travel and hotel accommodations.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated a 30-day notice period, which was changed to 15 days. The contradicted text contains the statement 'Bizzingo agrees to provide Joseph Theismann with at least fifteen (15) days' written notice'. While the changed text matches this sentence within the contradicted text, the initial original text (30 days' notice) now directly conflicts with the 15-day notice requirement established in the contradicted section, as well as the explanation stating the conflict. This is a clear inconsistency in obligations as defined by the perturbation type, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Theismann shall have free and full access thereto for such purposes and may make copies thereof. All books and records relative to Bizzingo's obligations hereunder shall be maintained and made accessible to Theismann for inspection at a location in the United States for at least one year after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Theismann shall have free and full access thereto for such purposes and may make copies thereof. All books and records relative to Bizzingo's obligations hereunder shall be maintained and made accessible to Theismann for inspection at a location in the United States for at least six months after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by modifying the period for maintaining and providing access to financial records. Section 6 now states that Bizzingo's books and records will be maintained and accessible for only 'six months' after termination, conflicting with the original 'one year' timeframe. This affects Theismann's ability to audit royalty payments post-termination, leading to uncertainty about the length of the audit period and potentially disadvantaging Theismann.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Theismann or his representatives, at his cost and expense, shall have the right, upon reasonable notice and during normal business hours, to inspect Bizzingo's books and records and all other documents and material in Bizzingo's possession or control with respect to the determination of Royalties payable hereunder. Theismann shall have free and full access thereto for such purposes and may make copies thereof. All books and records relative to Bizzingo's obligations hereunder shall be maintained and made accessible to Theismann for inspection at a location in the United States for at least six months after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the timeframe for maintaining and providing access to Bizzingo's books and records after termination from 'one year' to 'six months'. The contradicted text, which is actually the same section after the modification, also reflects the 'six months' timeframe. This creates a direct inconsistency with the original agreement, which specified a one-year period, now reduced to six months, impacting the ability of Theismann or his representatives to audit royalty payments post-termination effectively. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
